title: triticum ovatum subsp. ponticum
intro: Triticum ovatum subsp. ponticum, also known as Pontic wheat or Turkish wheat, is an ancient subspecies of wheat that is native to the Pontic Mountains in Turkey and other parts of the Middle East. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Triticum ovatum subsp. ponticum include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve blood sugar control, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
